threads: Телескоп Джеймс Уэбб смотрит так далеко, что видит время, когда Вселенной было всего 100 миллионов лет. Вместо галактик там, возможно, прячутся взрывы самых первых звёзд — парно-нестабильные сверхновые. Подтверждение откроет эру прямого наблюдения рождения космических светил.

title: Why Magnetic Fields Make Black Holes Quiet
oneliner: The thicker the disk around a black hole, the less often stars and their dense remnants collide inside it.
description: Researchers found: the thicker the disk around a black hole, the rarer objects collide. Magnetic fields puffing up the disk can cut merger rates by billions of times. Astrophysicists must rethink gravitational wave forecasts.
abstract: Disks of gas and dust swirl around giant black holes. It turns out their thickness crucially affects how often objects inside collide or capture each other. For example, when magnetic pressure is taken into account, the disk becomes 'puffier', and the frequency of such events drops by billions of billions of times — like the chance of meeting a friend in an empty hall versus a cramped room. So just how thick are these disks really?
threads: Астрономы считали, что окрестности чёрных дыр кипят от столкновений. Но всё меняет магнитное поле: оно делает диск толстым, и тогда звёзды почти не встречаются — оценки промахивались в триллионы раз. Тишина в центрах галактик оказалась куда глубже.

title: Catching an Interstellar Wanderer with a Solar Sling
oneliner: Scientists propose a risky plan: swoop in extremely close to the Sun to slingshot and overtake a fleeing interstellar object.
description: Interstellar object 3I/ATLAS is racing away, and a regular rocket can't catch it. But if you dive toward the Sun and fire the engines at peak speed, gravity acts like a giant sling. This maneuver gives a chance to reach the target in a few decades, but the probe must survive hellish heat.
abstract: Imagine trying to catch a train that’s already pulling away. But if you first dive extremely close to the Sun to get a massive speed boost, you just might catch it. That’s the trick — an Oberth solar maneuver — scientists have worked out for reaching 3I/ATLAS. Launch in 2035 and you could pull it off, though the journey takes 35–50 years. Makes you wonder: what else could we chase down like that in interstellar space?
threads: Межзвёздный гость 3I/ATLAS улетает от нас навсегда. Но если нырнуть к Солнцу на расстояние всего в 3,2 его радиуса и там врубить двигатели, можно рвануть вдогонку. Это не фантастика — расчёты показывают, что запуск в 2035 году даёт шанс через 35-50 лет увидеть вблизи кусочек чужой планетной системы.

title: The Red Dwarf Oxygen Trap
oneliner: In the race for light around red dwarfs, infrared bacteria pull ahead, leaving oxygenic photosynthesis in the dust.
description: Around dim stars like TRAPPIST-1, photosynthesis barely produces oxygen—visible light is scarce. Infrared bacteria, on the other hand, thrive, gobbling up nearly all the available energy. As a result, an oxygen atmosphere may never form, and complex life never arise.
abstract: Scientists calculated: on a planet around a dim star like TRAPPIST-1, photosynthesis would proceed hundreds of times slower due to the lack of the right light. Even over billions of years, too little oxygen would accumulate. Perhaps life there would remain invisible, without complex organisms — like a garden that never gets enough sun to bloom. Does that mean there are no forests or animals on such planets?
threads: Тусклый красный прожектор TRAPPIST-1 дает так мало видимого света, что кислородный фотосинтез едва тлеет. Зато бактерии с инфракрасным зрением захватывают планету. Кислородная атмосфера может не возникнуть, оставив мир без животных.

title: One membrane – two mysteries of the Universe
oneliner: A stretched membrane-diaphragm catches gravitational waves and senses dark matter.
description: A new type of detector uses a membrane inside an optical cavity to search for two substances at once: gravitational waves and dark matter. Light pressure stretches the membrane, and six such membranes cover frequencies from 0.5 to 40 kHz — from a low hum to a high squeak. Sensitivity to spacetime ri
threads: Крошечная мембрана, натянутая между зеркал, ловит рябь пространства — гравитационные волны. А если рядом расположить два разных материала, она почувствует и невидимую тёмную материю. Один прибор вместо двух.

title: The Stumbling Dance of Two Planets Around TOI-4495
oneliner: Two planets around the star TOI-4495 are nearly in sync, but their dance has stumbled, prompting a search for hidden causes.
description: Around TOI-4495, two planets orbit wrapped in thick gas blankets. Their orbits are almost tuned—two revolutions of the inner planet for one of the outer—but exact synchronization is absent. The elongated path of the inner planet persists for billions of years thanks to the unusual elasticity of its
threads: Две планеты у звезды TOI-4495 танцуют почти в унисон: пока внутренняя успевает сделать два круга, внешняя — только один. Но их шаг сбился, и внутреннюю планету слегка трясёт. Это может сохраниться только если внутри неё не слишком много тепла теряется.

title: Solar Storm Illuminated Magnetic Fields in Space
oneliner: For the first time, scientists have spotted how a solar storm twists the stream of ultra-high-energy particles, turning them into a remote probe for magnetic fields.
description: Usually, cosmic rays arrive evenly from all directions, but when the Sun ejects a magnetic cloud, their paths bend. On November 4, 2021, the LHAASO observatory caught a temporary lopsidedness, offering the first-ever glimpse of invisible magnetic structures in interplanetary space.
abstract: Cosmic rays are particles from deep space. Using an observatory in China, scientists noticed how magnetic storms on the Sun affect the streams of these particles near Earth, even at high energies. It's like ripples on water giving away a stone thrown into a pond: now we have a new way to 'see' invisible magnetic clouds in space.
threads: Солнечная буря 4 ноября 2021 года на несколько часов перекосила поток частиц‑тяжеловесов, летящих из глубин Вселенной. Энергия в тысячи раз выше света, а магнитное облако от Солнца изогнуло их пути, как ураганный ветер — струи дождя. Теперь такие «искривления» помогут ощупывать невидимые поля в космосе, словно эхолот.

title: A Lunar Telescope Will Reveal the Shadows of Distant Black Holes
oneliner: A telescope on the Moon will make the shadows of supermassive black holes in other galaxies visible.
description: Combining antennas on Earth and the Moon will deliver incredible sharpness. Astronomers have selected six black holes in alien galaxies whose silhouettes will become visible. The real twist: to glimpse the thinnest ring of light around them, we'll need antennas floating in space between planets.
threads: Лунный телескоп на обратной стороне превратит систему Земля–Луна в исполинскую антенну, способную улавливать тени чёрных дыр из чужих галактик. Чтобы их увидеть, учёные отобрали шесть подходящих целей. Самое удивительное: можно будет разглядеть даже фотонное кольцо — тончайший световой обруч вокруг чернильного пятна — и проверить теорию гравитации там, где она работает на износ.

title: The Ultimate Stiffness Limit of Neutron Stars
oneliner: The ratio of pressure to energy density in neutron stars cannot exceed 0.385 — that's the universal limit.
description: In a neutron star, matter is compressed to the limit. But even that limit has a limit: physicists have calculated that the ratio of pressure to energy density never exceeds 0.385. This law holds for any ultra-dense matter in the universe.
abstract: The center of the most massive neutron star is squeezed tighter than anything else in the Universe. Scientists have figured out just how much matter can be compressed before the star collapses. It turns out that pressure cannot exceed 38.5% of the energy density — it's like a spring compressed almost to its limit, but not quite. Curious, what would happen if you added just a tiny bit more?
threads: Нейтронная звезда спрессовывает гору до крупинки, но даже там материя не беспредельна: у давления и плотности есть строгий верхний порог. Оказалось, их отношение не превышает 0,385. Это число перекраивает понимание сверхплотной материи.

title: Dark Dance: How Energy and Matter Swap Roles
oneliner: A new study shows dark energy and dark matter exchange energy, and the direction of exchange has changed over time.
description: Eight models of interaction between dark energy and dark matter fit observations better than the standard theory. Surprisingly, in the past, dark energy density could have been negative—like an energy debt in the dance. The finding refines the history of the expansion of the universe.
threads: Тёмная энергия и тёмная материя не просто соседи — они обмениваются энергией, как танцоры в космическом танго. Но иногда один партнёр отдаёт больше, чем имеет, и баланс нарушается. Это может изменить наше понимание расширения Вселенной.

title: Thin Disks of Galaxies Already Existed in the Young Universe
oneliner: Pictures from the James Webb telescope showed that flat, pancake-like stellar disks appeared way earlier than we thought.
description: Astronomers studied distant galaxies when the Universe was just a few billion years old. They developed a new way to more accurately measure the thickness of stellar disks, even if they are slightly tilted. It turns out that thin disks, similar to our Milky Way, had already formed by that early time
abstract: Galaxies like our Milky Way are flat disks of stars. Using the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers looked at very distant galaxies to measure how thick these disks were long ago. They found that already 11 billion years ago, galaxies had thin disks similar to ones today. Imagine stacking a few pancakes — that’s how flat they are, challenging ideas that they puffed up over time.
threads: Галактики-блины в ранней Вселенной. Телескоп Уэбба показал, что тонкие звёздные диски существовали уже 11 миллиардов лет назад. Раньше думали, что они толстеют со временем, а теперь выходит наоборот: сначала появились изящные «блинчики», а пухлые толстые диски наросли позже. Это меняет картину взросления галактик.

title: Wind for the swift: how turbulence heats the solar corona
oneliner: Chaotic electric fields in plasma, like a sieve, let only fast particles through, creating 'tails' of superspeeds and heating the solar corona to millions of degrees.
description: The theory discovered: chaotic electric fields in plasma, like a sieve, sift fast particles from slow ones, forming a universal 'tail' of super-fast particles. The reason lies in the shielding effect: slow particles wrap themselves in charge clouds and become invisible to the field, while fast ones
threads: В плазме, как в игре «Царь горы», быстрые частицы получают дополнительный разгон от турбулентности, а медленные остаются в тени. Именно это, возможно, греет солнечную корону до миллионов градусов — загадка, мучившая физиков 80 лет.

title: Dark Matter Is Born from Ghostly Neutrinos
oneliner: Sterile neutrinos may have arisen in the first moments after the Big Bang and become dark matter.
description: Scientists have shown how sterile neutrinos were born in the hot young Universe. This explains why we don't see their X-ray glow and turns dark matter into a tool for measuring the temperature of the early cosmos.
abstract: It turns out dark matter might consist of sterile neutrinos—particles that hardly interact with ordinary matter. They are born in the hot 'furnace' of the early Universe when the particle that inflated space decays. This explains why we don't see their X-ray glow. Imagine: invisible mass hidden in the cosmic fire—what else can it tell us about the birth of the world?
threads: Тёмная материя могла возникнуть в первые мгновения после Большого взрыва, когда пространство молниеносно расширилось и раскалилось. Частицы-невидимки, стерильные нейтрино, могут сохранить память о том древнем пожаре и выдать себя слабым рентгеновским свечением.

title: Black Holes 'Weigh' the Expansion Rate of the Universe
oneliner: Three characteristic masses of merging black holes allowed a more precise measurement of the universe's expansion rate.
description: Analysis of 137 black hole mergers revealed three favorite masses: around 10, 18, and 33 solar masses. A flexible analysis, like a tuner, picked out these 'notes' more precisely than previous methods. As a result, the expansion rate of the universe was refined by 21%. This shows that the better we h
abstract: Gravitational waves from black hole mergers carry information about the masses of the objects. Their distribution is like a 'fingerprint' of the universe, which can be used to measure the expansion rate. A new analysis of 137 events revealed three mass peaks (around 10, 18, and 33 solar masses) and made it possible to determine the Hubble constant more precisely. So, can the 'shape' of black holes tell us about the fate of the cosmos?
threads: Чёрные дыры падают в космическую паутину не случайно: у них есть любимые размеры — 10, 18 и 33 солнца. По этим «нотам» можно услышать, как быстро раздувается Вселенная, и даже расслышать тёмную энергию.

title: Dance of the Invisible Giants: Gaia Finds a 'Desert' of Brown Dwarfs
oneliner: Gaia telescope finds ten times more brown dwarfs lurking around Sun-like stars on close orbits than previously thought — the 'desert' was a mirage.
description: Astronomers are reimagining the map of nearby stellar pairs. The zone of tight orbits around Sun-like stars was once called the 'brown dwarf desert,' thought to hold few objects heavier than planets but lighter than stars. Fresh Gaia data reveal that many stars are dancing with unseen companions — a
abstract: For a long time, astronomers thought that brown dwarfs (bodies between planets and stars in mass) are almost never found near sun-like stars — as if there's a 'desert' in this part of space. But a new analysis of Gaia telescope data shows that actually every tenth neighboring star home could have such a hidden companion. Imagine: around every tenth star, as in a child's game of hide-and-seek, a massive, unseen companion is hiding.
threads: Раньше была пустыня, а теперь — сад: телескоп Gaia нашёл, что у каждой десятой звезды типа Солнца может быть невидимый сосед — коричневый карлик. Это в десять раз больше, чем думали. Значит, наши представления о рождении планет требуют пересмотра.

title: The Icy Magnets of Uranus and Neptune
oneliner: Temperature differences inside the ice giants turn ordinary ice into a magnetic generator.
description: Deep inside Uranus and Neptune, monstrous pressure transforms ice into a substance where an oxygen framework is threaded with flowing protons. Temperature differences within the planet set these protons in motion, generating electric current and a magnetic field. This explains the weird magnetospher
abstract: Uranus and Neptune are ice giants with really weird magnetic fields. Turns out, deep inside them, water is in a special 'superionic' state (kind of like ice and liquid at the same time), and temperature differences make charged particles move, creating magnetism. So, almost like a battery, heat turns into a magnetic field. So why then are the magnetic fields of these planets so different?
threads: На Уране и Нептуне лёд работает как батарейка: перепады температуры заставляют протоны течь, создавая магнитные поля. Это объясняет загадку их разной силы и странных форм.

title: Distant Quasar Sets Gamma-Ray Record
oneliner: The MAGIC telescopes caught a record-breaking gamma-ray burst from a black hole that lived 7 billion years ago.
description: Astronomers observed a gamma-ray flare from a quasar — the ultra-bright core of a galaxy with a black hole inside. The light traveled for 7 billion years. Unexpectedly, it turned out to have originated not from the hole itself, but from the dust ring surrounding it. This helps us understand how blac
abstract: Astronomers detected a gamma-ray flare from a distant quasar — a cosmic beacon whose light traveled billions of years to reach us. It quickly faded, and the spectral cutoff at 50 GeV suggested the emission comes from fast particles colliding with radiation from the dusty "doughnut" around a black hole. How far can such rays reach?
threads: В декабре 2017 года телескопы MAGIC заметили, как далекий квазар вспыхнул гамма-лучами, став в 50 раз ярче Крабовидной туманности. Свет шел 7 миллиардов лет от черной дыры, окутанной пылью. Такие вспышки — редкие окна в эпоху, когда Вселенная была вдвое моложе.

title: Radio signal from space rang like a bell: rhythmic fading detected
oneliner: A radio signal from space rang like a fading bell, revealing the trembling of a magnetic cocoon around the ultra-dense core of a dead star.
description: A powerful radio pulse from a distant galaxy revealed eight fading bursts—like a cosmic bell that tolled and then went silent. This is the first observation of fading rhythms in such signals, and it reveals the trembling of the magnetic cocoon around the ultra-dense core of a dead star. From the rhy
threads: Радиовсплеск из далёкой галактики прозвенел как космический колокольчик: восемь импульсов затухали ровно, словно кто-то дёрнул струну. Это может быть первый «ритм сердца» нейтронной звезды — редкая улика, приближающая нас к разгадке тайны FRB.

title: Cosmic Mirages Test the Law of Gravity
oneliner: Distorted light from distant galaxies helped test gravity theories that could replace dark energy.
description: The accelerated expansion of the universe is usually explained by dark energy, but its nature is unknown. Perhaps it doesn’t exist, and gravity on cosmic scales deviates from Einstein’s law. Using maps of weak lensing—subtle distortions in the shapes of galaxies—scientists tested modified gravity th
abstract: The Universe is expanding faster and faster, and scientists are checking if the laws of gravity have been altered. They studied how light from distant galaxies gets distorted, much like how ripples on water change the view of the bottom. It turned out that some theories don't contradict previous data but offer new clues. Will we be able to unravel the cause of the acceleration this way?
threads: Свет далёких галактик искажается, как отражение в кривом зеркале. Это слабое линзирование помогает понять, что движет расширением Вселенной: невидимая тёмная энергия или сама гравитация работает иначе. Новый анализ показывает: поправки к закону Ньютона могут объяснить ускорение, и найдены их допустимые пределы.

title: How a Crystal’s Hidden Order Sharpens Gamma-Ray Telescopes
oneliner: A slight crystal tilt—within a hair’s width—transforms it into a super-efficient gamma-ray detector, enabling lighter space telescopes.
description: Gamma rays are hard to catch. In a telescope’s crystal detector, they create a particle shower that usually fans out, demanding heavy hardware. But if the crystal’s atomic grid aligns with the ray, the shower tightens. A small, oriented crystal then does a better job, even measuring the gamma ray’s
threads: High-energy gamma rays from space vanish in a flash, but a trick with crystal alignment turns that flash into a fountain of particles, making them easier to catch. This new telescope design could sniff out dark matter.

title: Invisible Vortices Sculpt Supermassive Black Holes
oneliner: Vibrations in an invisible field spawn swirling particles of dark light, driving clouds of dark matter into supermassive black holes at breakneck speed.
description: In the early universe, giant black holes were born at a dizzying pace. The key lies in a dance of invisible fields: the trembling of the axion field and dark photons created vortex mixers that prevented matter clouds from fragmenting and whisked away their spin. Thus, quantum crumbs sculpted the see
threads: Невидимые аксионные поля в ранней Вселенной породили вихри из тёмных фотонов. Эти космические миксеры помогли облакам тёмной материи сжаться сразу в гигантские чёрные дыры. Так крошечные квантовые дрожания могли стать семенами самых массивных монстров, озадачивающих астрономов.

title: Сверхновая из детства Вселенной
oneliner: Космическая линза из скопления галактик помогла разглядеть сверхновую из детства Вселенной.
description: Самую далёкую сверхновую SN Eos подтвердили, разложив её свет в спектр. Взрыв произошёл в среде, почти лишённой тяжёлых элементов, — прямое доказательство рождения массивных звёзд в ранней Вселенной. Открытие стало возможным только благодаря гравитационному линзированию и поможет уточнить темп рожде
threads: Самая далёкая сверхновая, обнаруженная благодаря космической линзе, показывает: массивные звёзды рождались уже в младенческой Вселенной. Это заставит пересмотреть раннюю историю космоса.

title: How Weak Gravity Does Without Dark Matter
oneliner: Scientists have found a way to explain galaxy rotation by changing the law of gravity on the outskirts—no dark matter required.
description: This approach rewrites gravity's rules where it's barely felt—at the edges of galaxies. Under normal conditions, it aligns with Einstein's theory, but for weak fields, it adds a tweak. The solution needs no new particles and explains why galaxies spin faster than their visible mass permits.
threads: На окраинах галактик гравитация настолько слаба, что начинает вести себя не как обычно, а подчиняется странной симметрии, связанной с размером всей Вселенной. Новая теория объясняет это без темной материи и, возможно, изменит наше понимание космоса.

title: The Secret of Neutron Stars: Sizing Them Up
oneliner: A new method directly links the mass and radius of a neutron star to its internal structure—without any extra hypotheses.
description: Neutron stars are the densest objects in the universe. Their internal makeup has long been a mystery. A new approach uses mass and radius to pinpoint what’s inside, without relying on assumptions.
abstract: Scientists have come up with a new way to figure out what’s inside neutron stars without peeking inside—just by using their observed mass and radius. It’s like determining the filling of a sealed pie by measuring its weight and size alone. This method turned out to be more reliable and faster than previous ones, especially for stars with small radii. What other secrets do neutron stars hold?
threads: Как узнать, что внутри нейтронной звезды? Учёные придумали новый способ: смотреть прямо на её размер и массу, а не гадать о свойствах материи. Это всё равно что оценивать упругость мяча, сжимая его, вместо того чтобы лезть внутрь.

title: Neutrino Decay Reconciles Two Worlds
oneliner: Neutrino decay into invisible particles explains the discrepancy between cosmological and laboratory data on mass.
description: Cosmology saw neutrinos as lighter than fluff, while lab experiments demanded noticeable weight. Decay into light invisibles resolves the conflict: some of the mass simply vanishes over time. Ordinary neutrinos, however, aren't fit for such a trick.
abstract: Cosmological observations say the total mass of all neutrino types is tiny, while oscillation experiments require it to be noticeably larger. This contradiction can be resolved if neutrinos decay over time into lighter, invisible particles — like vanishing guests at a party. This hypothesis lifts the mass constraints and reconciles the data. So, could neutrinos be heavier than we thought?
threads: Нейтрино могут распадаться на невидимые частицы, и это решает главный спор об их массе. Космологи считали, что нейтрино очень лёгкие, а физики требовали большей массы. Распад объясняет, куда делась разница: часть массы просто исчезла со временем, как картошка из дырявого мешка.

title: Galactic Bubbles: How Black Holes Paint the Cosmos
oneliner: New simulation explains the nature of mysterious bubbles in the Circinus galaxy.
description: Simulations reveal that giant bubbles in the Circinus galaxy are sculpted by narrow black hole jets, not stellar winds. This unlocks the secret of how black holes govern galaxy growth.
abstract: Galactic bubbles are like soap bubbles: they are blown by jets from the central black hole. Simulations show that this is exactly how the mysterious bubbles in the neighboring Circinus galaxy formed. This means that the huge Fermi bubbles in our own Galaxy could have formed in the same way. Imagine an invisible force blowing up cosmic clouds—and reshaping the entire galaxy!
threads: В центре почти каждой крупной галактики спит гигантская чёрная дыра. Иногда она просыпается и выдувает пузыри плазмы, сравнимые по размерам с самой галактикой. Новое моделирование показало, что эти пузыри формируются узкими джетами, словно раскалённое пламя паяльника в масле. Поняв их форму, мы сможем предсказывать, как чёрные дыры управляют рождением и смертью целых звёздных систем.

title: A Trap for Invisible Axions: A Magnetic Detector
oneliner: Scientists built a supersensitive detector to find dark matter particles, but so far they've only caught silence.
description: Physicists are hunting axions—light particles that might make up dark matter. They built a device with a levitating magnetized rod that should tremble if axions fly through it. In a new experiment, the rod stayed still, but the scientists set record constraints on how strongly axions interact with o
threads: Крошечный магнит парил в вакууме, как стрелка заколдованного компаса. Учёные ждали, что невидимый ветер тёмной материи качнёт его. Ветер не появился, зато физики поняли: он должен быть ещё слабее. Теперь строят детектор в сотни раз чувствительнее.

title: A Tiny Tweak to Inflation Theory Explains a Cosmic Anomaly
oneliner: A new perspective on the ripples of the newborn Universe, with a tiny addition, solves the mystery of the cosmic 'quiet zone'.
description: Generalized equations of the early Universe with a minimal correction better describe the 'quiet' large-scale patterns in ancient light maps, without disturbing the finely tuned small-scale picture. This hints at a gap in the standard inflation model.
threads: Ранняя Вселенная раздулась как пузырь, оставив рябь. Но на карте реликтового излучения зияет странная «тихая зона». Новая теория с крошечной поправочкой (всего 0,0001) объясняет эту загадку, не ломая стандартную модель. Возможно, мы нащупали кончик нити, ведущей к квантовой гравитации.

title: Gravitational waves refine cosmic expansion rate
oneliner: Combining gravitational wave signals with galaxy surveys yields a more precise measurement of how fast the universe is growing.
description: By combining gravitational waves from merging neutron stars with galaxy surveys, scientists refined the universe’s expansion rate to 67.9 km/s per megaparsec (6.4% uncertainty), bridging a long-standing debate.
abstract: For the first time, scientists combined gravitational wave signals from merging neutron stars with data on how galaxies are distributed and bend light. This allowed them to measure the expansion rate of the Universe: 68 km/s per megaparsec. The new method could solve the puzzle of why different measurements give different speeds.
threads: Two ultradense stars collided and sent tremors through space. By matching those shivers with a map of millions of galaxies, scientists have just clocked the universe’s growth spurt with record precision. The result: 67.9 km/s per megaparsec—hinting at a way to solve the biggest argument in cosmology.

title: Smashing a moon slowed down the main asteroid
oneliner: After DART rammed its moon, a day on asteroid Didymos stretched by 0.18 seconds.
description: The DART probe's impact with moon Dimorphos caused the main asteroid to spin slower. Measurements showed that Didymos's day lengthened by 0.18 seconds, and the asteroid itself became more flattened. It's the first time a kinetic impactor has reshaped and respun a celestial body.
threads: Казалось бы, врезаться на скорости 6 км/с в 160-метровый булыжник и чуть изменить его орбиту — уже успех. Но астрономы пошли дальше: они заметили, что главный астероид Дидим закрутился медленнее на доли секунды. Удар изменил его форму, сделав более приплюснутым. Как фигурист, который разводит руки, чтобы замедлить вращение. Теперь мы знаем: космический «толчок» может не только отклонить опасный объект, но и неожиданно изменить его собственное движение.

title: Wobbly Magnetars: The Secret Behind Bizarre Radio Flashes
oneliner: Fast radio bursts are born on lopsided magnetars, whose shaky rotation hides and reveals their beams.
description: Brief powerful radio signals from space behave oddly: some repeat, others are one-offs. A new explanation: their source is misshapen magnetars. The star spins like a lopsided top, its radio beam flickering toward us, then vanishing for years. This same crookedness blocks the detection of gravitation
threads: Астрономы терялись в догадках: почему одни космические радиосигналы повторяются, а другие — нет? Разгадка нашлась у кривых магнитаров. Если бы Земля была такой же неровной, нас бы швыряло, как на сломанной карусели. Но именно эта «корявость» прячет гравитационное эхо от наших приборов.

title: How Giant Black Holes Are Born in the Early Universe
oneliner: New simulations show that supermassive black holes grow from heavy seeds just 700 million years after the Big Bang.
description: A computer simulation of the early universe revealed: supermassive black holes are born from plump seeds with a million solar masses and grow into giants in mere hundreds of millions of years, explaining the mysterious 'little red dots' seen by the Webb telescope.
threads: В ранней Вселенной пылевые коконы скрывают гигантов: внутри них зародыши чёрных дыр массой в миллион Солнц быстро растут, поглощая газ. Именно эти объекты телескоп JWST видит как загадочные 'красные точки' — мгновение перед рождением сверхмассивных монстров.

title: Dark Energy Controls the Weight of Dark Matter
oneliner: Dark energy and dark matter exchange energy like dance partners, and this resolves cosmic mysteries.
description: Scientists have proposed that dark energy affects the mass of dark matter particles. This energy exchange accurately reproduces the observed expansion of the Universe and alters the growth of galaxy clusters by 10% — a difference that new telescopes will test. If the connection is confirmed, our und
threads: Неожиданный образ: тёмная энергия и тёмная материя меняются ролями в космическом танце. Их скрытая связь объясняет странности расширения Вселенной и оставляет следы в расположении галактик — будущие телескопы смогут это проверить.

title: Why Cold Stars Lead Scientists Astray
oneliner: Standard models of starlight don't work for ultracool dwarfs.
description: The light of TRAPPIST-1 revealed that standard models were wrong: in its cold atmosphere, molecules collide differently, hiding the chemical composition. Refining the rules will help us better understand the star itself and its planets.
abstract: Astronomers have found that stellar models, like out-of-tune musical instruments, don't correctly reproduce the 'melody' of light from the ultracool star TRAPPIST-1. It turns out, it's all about gas pressure in the atmosphere—if we 'dial it down', the model matches observations. This will help us better study planets around such stars. Wonder what other 'notes' red dwarfs are hiding?
threads: Свет холодной звезды ТРАППИСТ-1 оказался обманчивым: привычные модели астрономов предсказывали в нём сильные следы железа и воды, а телескопы их почти не видят. Всё дело в том, как молекулы толкаются в разреженной атмосфере — гораздо мягче, чем у Солнца. Пересмотр правил игры поможет точнее искать жизнь на планетах у таких звёзд.

title: Black Holes as Unexpected Beacons of the Early Universe
oneliner: New research reveals that supermassive black holes emitted 3–4 times more ultraviolet light than previously thought, playing a key role in the reionization of the universe.
description: It turns out that black holes were 3–4 times brighter in ultraviolet light than scientists believed. This raises their contribution to the clearing of the universe to 20%, and possibly higher when dim nuclei are considered. A new model of active galactic nuclei changes the view of reionization.
threads: Неожиданный факт: скромные чёрные дыры в сердцах галактик оказались намного ярче в ультрафиолете, чем считали астрономы. Они генерируют в 3-4 раза больше ионизующего излучения, что делает их значимыми игроками в эпоху реионизации — когда Вселенная стала прозрачной. Возможно, именно эти древние «маяки» пробили тьму.

title: Fireworks Called Off: Star Shrouded in Smoke
oneliner: James Webb found that a faded star in Andromeda didn't become a black hole but hid behind a thick dust cloud, like a dud firework.
description: Some massive stars die without an explosion, quietly collapsing into a black hole. James Webb Space Telescope observations of the suspicious star M31-2014-DS1 showed it didn't vanish: a dusty cloud glows in its place with no X-rays. Possibly it's not a solo death but the merger of two stars shrouded
abstract: In 2014, a distant star flared up and then almost faded away—astronomers suspected it had quietly turned into a black hole. But the James Webb Space Telescope, a decade later, spotted its warm 'breath' in infrared light, hidden behind a thick veil of dust, like a smoldering campfire behind a heavy curtain. So what is it: a failed stellar death or a cosmic merger?
threads: В 2014 году звезда в Андромеде вспыхнула и пропала. Спустя 10 лет Уэбб нашёл там тепло: она спряталась за пылью. Это не чёрная дыра, а укутанная звезда — редкая «тихая смерть» без взрыва.

title: Cosmic Fireflies: How Dark Matter Turns into Visible Light
oneliner: Invisible clouds of dark matter flash like fireflies when they enter a magnetic field.
description: Dark matter may consist of invisible particles that clump into clouds. Once such a cloud finds itself in a magnetic field, it lights up with ordinary light — like a firefly in the night. This effect offers a new way to detect the mysterious substance that makes up 85% of the Universe.
threads: Невидимые сгустки тёмной материи, похожие на облака-призраки, могут вспыхивать светом в магнитном поле — как светлячки в ночи. Этот эффект открывает новый способ охоты на тёмную материю, которая составляет 85% массы Вселенной.

title: How One Supernova United Different Types of Explosions
oneliner: A superluminous supernova showed that the differences between such explosions are merely a matter of temperature, not distinct natures.
description: Supernova PTF12dam changed its appearance right as it was brightening. This prompted astronomers to think: the entire diversity of superluminous supernovae could be explained by the temperature of the ejected material at peak brightness. It turned out that the slower the light rises, the weaker the
threads: Одна сверхновая удивила астрономов: пока разгоралась, она сменила свой «паспортный» тип. Оказалось, не нужно выдумывать разные виды — всё дело в температуре вспышки. Теперь, зная это, мы лучше понимаем, как работают «звёздные моторы» в центре таких взрывов. Это ещё один шаг к разгадке последних дней массивных звёзд.

title: New Arid Meteor Shower: How a YouTube Camera Helped the Discovery
oneliner: For the first time, scientists and hundreds of volunteers used a YouTube camera to confirm the appearance of the predicted Arid meteor shower.
description: Hunting for a rare celestial phenomenon is like setting up a camera trap in a dense forest. In the fall of 2021, astronomers aimed a camera with high light sensitivity at the dome of the Subaru Telescope into the empty sky above Maunakea, trying to catch the predicted but elusive Arid meteor shower.
abstract: Imagine a comet leaving behind a trail of dust, and when Earth flies through it, we see 'shooting stars.' In 2021, scientists first noticed the new Arid meteor shower using an ordinary webcam in Hawaii. This discovery shows how even amateurs can help science. What other cosmic surprises are hidden in dust clouds?
threads: Предсказанный поток Ариды проявился на камере, которую смотрели любители по всему миру. Комета Финлей выбросила пыль на огромной скорости, и вот — слабые метеоры зажглись в небе. Неожиданное шоу, открытое не телескопом, а живым взглядом.

title: Planet's Air: Recipe from a Star
oneliner: Gases with carbon in the atmospheres of lifeless planets depend on the star and distance, astronomers have found.
description: The study showed: at the edges of habitable zones, carbon monoxide accumulates to dangerous levels due to a breakdown in chemistry. And formaldehyde, important for life, forms better around hot stars. This will help select planets for detailed study.
threads: Оказывается, на планетах без жизни угарный газ может накопиться до смертельных уровней просто из-за слабого света звезды. А формальдегид — основа для будущих живых клеток — лучше всего рождается у горячих звёзд. Теперь телескопы смогут угадывать, где прячутся ингредиенты жизни, всего лишь по составу воздуха.

title: A black hole tore a star apart and sent a ghost particle
oneliner: Astronomers have for the first time linked a mysterious high-energy neutrino to the moment a black hole ripped a star apart.
description: A single neutrino, caught by an Antarctic telescope, coincided with a flare from a star being torn apart by a black hole. The discovery showed that such catastrophes give birth to ghost particles. Now we know one of the sources of cosmic neutrinos.
threads: Звезда подошла слишком близко к чёрной дыре и была разорвана на части. Этот космический обед породил неуловимую частицу-призрак — нейтрино, которое пролетело миллиарды лет и застряло во льдах Антарктиды. Теперь астрономы нашли место преступления. Возможно, так рождается большая часть загадочных нейтрино высоких энергий.

title: Dark Matter and Dark Energy: A Slow Reaction
oneliner: Scientists described the transformation of dark matter and dark energy as a reversible chemical reaction resulting from collision physics.
description: A new model shows that dark matter and dark energy can transform into each other like a reversible chemical reaction. Observational tests confirmed that this process is almost impossible — only a few thousand transformations have occurred in the entire history of the Universe.
abstract: Scientists have proposed a new model where dark matter and dark energy can transform into each other, like ice and water. This has helped measure the expansion rate of the universe more precisely — about 67.7 km/s per megaparsec. Perhaps the dark world is far more lively than we thought?
threads: Космос полон невидимых компонентов — тёмной материи и тёмной энергии. Новая модель показала: они могут превращаться друг в друга, как лёд тает в воду, но с одним условием — это происходит невероятно редко. Если бы такое взаимодействие шло быстрее, ночное небо выглядело бы иначе. Теперь учёные понимают, почему мы до сих пор не поймали ни одного такого события — оно прячется в строгих природных запретах.

title: A black hole rings nonlinearly: new gravitational overtones heard
oneliner: Spacetime after black hole mergers rings more intricately: nonlinear gravitational wave overtones discovered.
description: In the gravitational waves from the black hole merger GW250114, nonlinear overtones—vibrations born from the interaction of the fundamental tones—have been directly detected for the first time. This discovery proves the complex nature of strong gravity and provides a new way to test relativity.
abstract: Scientists have heard, for the first time, an 'echo' from the collision of two black holes — faint secondary waves, like ripples on water after a stone is thrown. This echo shows how the newborn black hole trembles and settles down, hinting at gravity’s surprising properties. What other music do black holes hide?
threads: При слиянии чёрных дыр пространство-время дрожит, как колокол. Но новый анализ показал: дрожь не простая — в ней прячутся ноты, рождённые от трения самих колебаний. Открытие сулит способ заглянуть в самое сердце искривлённой гравитации.

title: The Quiet Death of a Star
oneliner: A star in the Andromeda galaxy disappeared without a bang, leaving just a cold dust cloud — that’s how invisible black holes come into being.
description: In the Andromeda galaxy, a star faded away without a flash. James Webb and the Chandra telescope saw a dim object in a dust cocoon. This proves that a black hole can be born in the quiet collapse of a star, not just in the fire of a supernova.
abstract: Imagine a star simply switched off, like a light bulb. That's what happened in Andromeda: star M31-2014-DS1 went dark, and in its place now sits a black hole. Observations show the stellar remains slowly fading, like embers of a campfire. What exactly is making it dim so much?
threads: В галактике Андромеды звезда просто исчезла без вспышки. Телескопы увидели тусклый объект в облаке пыли — так рождается чёрная дыра, когда звезда тихо «выдыхает» оболочку и схлопывается. Космический призрак.

title: Gravitational Waves from the Boiling Universe: A New Precise Method
oneliner: A new method frees gravitational wave calculations from coordinate arbitrariness, bringing us closer to the echo of cosmic boiling.
description: Cosmic 'boiling' in the early universe spawned bubbles of new vacuum and gravitational waves. Previous calculations of these waves were flawed due to their dependence on the frame of reference. A new method, borrowed from neutrino physics, eliminates this arbitrariness and promises a clearer picture
abstract: In the early Universe, the quantum vacuum could bubble up with a new phase — like water boiling in a kettle. Previously, calculations of this process depended on the observer’s ‘point of view’ (gauge). Now, it’s been shown how to achieve an objective picture, to more accurately predict gravitational waves. What story might this cosmic echo tell us?
threads: В первые мгновения Вселенная могла «закипеть», породив гравитационные волны — дрожь самого пространства. Долгое время расчёты этих волн путались в математических условностях. Новый подход очищает вычисления от лишней зависимости, позволяя услышать истинный голос космоса.

title: How Dark Matter Was Born from the Geometry of the Universe
oneliner: The geometry of the Universe could have acted like a mixer, whipping up dark matter from the energy of expansion.
description: A new model shows how dark matter—a dust-like, invisible substance—could have emerged from the geometry of space during the rapid expansion. The mechanism works without fine-tuning: the right amount of matter automatically results from the expansion rate. After matter came to dominate over radiation
abstract: It turns out dark matter could have appeared on its own during inflation—when the Universe expanded in a flash. Like an invisible conductor, a special field tuned the amount of matter so that galaxies could form later. How many more surprises does the early Universe hold?
threads: В первые доли секунды Вселенная бешено раздувалась. Учёные выяснили: благодаря особой геометрии в этой горячке могла родиться тёмная материя — невидимая пыль, из которой позже собрались галактики. Это объясняет, почему её так много.

title: Tiny Black Holes Rip Atoms Apart
oneliner: Tiny black holes rip hydrogen atoms apart with gravity, like taffy, and that tearing reveals their invisible nature.
description: Scientists have shown that atom-sized black holes might be dark matter. With their gravity, they rip apart hydrogen atoms, leaving flashes. These traces can be found in ancient radio waves and chemical anomalies — that's how the invisible mass will be discovered.
abstract: Imagine tiny black holes, no heavier than an asteroid, making up the invisible dark matter. Their gravity is so fierce it can tear atoms apart, even splitting nuclei, much like a vicious tide ripping a boat to pieces. Could this be the key to finally spotting them?
threads: Крошечная чёрная дыра пролетает сквозь облако водорода и растягивает атомы, как ириску. Эта идея может привести к открытию тёмного вещества — невидимой материи, скрепляющей галактики.

title: Cosmic Ruler: Inflection Point Instead of Peak
oneliner: Astrophysicists have found a new reference point in galaxy distribution — an inflection point that remains unchanged even after billions of years.
description: After the Big Bang, sound waves left a rhythmic pattern in the distribution of galaxies — a 'standard ruler.' But over time, gravity blurs the sharp peak on the distance graph. Scientists discovered that the point of steepest slope on this graph hardly changes and serves as a more reliable tool. Ver
abstract: Scientists have found a new way to measure distances in the Universe, using special points in the distribution of galaxies — the 'linear point'. This method is more accurate than the old one because it suffers less from distortions. Imagine that instead of a blurred shadow, you use the sharp outline of an object to determine its size more precisely. It's intriguing: could this change our understanding of the cosmos?
threads: Песчаная дюна подсказала астрономам новую космическую линейку. Как у дюны самый крутой склон устойчивее вершины, так и на графике распределения галактик особая «точка перегиба» меньше размывается временем. Этот метод, проверенный на данных DESI, обещает точнее измерить тёмную энергию и понять, куда движется Вселенная.

title: How Giant Planets Kept Their Rapid Spin
oneliner: Astronomers have figured out why giant planets spin much faster than more massive brown dwarfs.
description: Measurements of 32 celestial bodies revealed that giant planets spin almost at the brink of tearing apart because their gaseous cocoon barely slowed them down in their youth. Brown dwarfs, on the contrary, lost spin due to a denser disk. Moreover, planets don't depend on proximity to a star, while c
abstract: Scientists measured the rotation speeds of distant giant planets and brown dwarfs (failed stars). It turned out that planets spin faster than similar-mass brown dwarfs—it's like comparing a swift spinning top to a slow heavy flywheel. Perhaps the difference is linked to how they were born: for planets, the braking disk around them was weaker. Think about it: what governs the cosmic dance of giants?
threads: Планеты-гиганты вращаются почти на пределе разрыва, а коричневые карлики — в разы медленнее. Астрономы выяснили: всё дело в слабом «дисковом тормозе» при рождении. Планетам почти не мешали раскрутиться. Это меняет взгляд на юность планетных систем.

title: How Young Stars' Rotation Destroys Their Magnetic Field
oneliner: The rapid rotation of a young star doesn't strengthen its magnetic field—it tears it apart.
description: Computer models show that when a star's inner layers spin faster than its outer ones, its large-scale magnetic field collapses into chaos. This explains the variety of magnetic fields in mature stars.
abstract: Young stars, much like infants, change quickly on the inside: the core shrinks and the outer layers rotate at different speeds. This difference can ‘snap’ their mighty magnets, turning strong fields into weak, wobbly ones. It shows why stars that look alike can glow so differently. Picture a star losing its compass in an inner whirl—what shows it the way then?
threads: Молодые звёзды крутятся так, что их внутренности обгоняют внешние слои. Это словно миксер, который безжалостно перекручивает магнитные линии, ломая простое поле на куски. Результат — тот самый «магнитный беспорядок», который мы видим у взрослых звёзд.

title: Red Eyes: Cosmic Lens Reveals a Pair of Black Holes
oneliner: The James Webb Space Telescope spotted a pair of red "eyes" in a distant galaxy — revealed by a gravitational lens.
description: Using a gravitational lens — a massive cluster of galaxies bending light — the James Webb Space Telescope detected two red dots in a distant galaxy. These are intermediate-mass black holes, located not in the center but on the outskirts. The discovery suggests such invisible objects might be widespr
abstract: In a distant galaxy, magnified by a gravitational lens, astronomers noticed a pair of faint red dots – the 'Red Eyes'. They are located not in the center but on the outskirts, and could be seeds of intermediate-mass black holes. Like two fireflies in a huge forest, glimpsed through a strong magnifying glass, they hint that such objects are everywhere. How many secrets does an ordinary galaxy hold?
threads: Астрономы нашли в далёкой галактике две пары красных «глаз» — это растущие чёрные дыры, спрятанные так хорошо, что без гравитационной линзы их не разглядеть. Оказалось, они блуждают на окраине, а не в центре. Значит, галактики могут кишеть такими невидимками, которые потом сливаются в сверхмассивного монстра.

title: A Cosmic Lighthouse Born from a Star's Explosion
oneliner: In a distant galaxy, after a star explosion, astronomers may be witnessing the birth of a pulsar — a cosmic lighthouse, possibly for the first time.
description: Years after a supernova flash, astronomers noticed a growing radio signal. By combining several telescopes into a giant antenna, they spotted a tiny, almost stationary source. Everything points to a young pulsar — a spinning neutron star that, like a lighthouse, beams narrow jets of radiation. If co
abstract: Years after its explosion, supernova SN 2012au unexpectedly 'lit up' again in radio waves. Scientists figured out: it's likely the 'breathing' of a newborn pulsar — a rapidly spinning neutron star, like a lighthouse in space. This is the first time such an object has been caught outside our Galaxy. Wonder what other surprises old supernovae might be hiding?
threads: Через 10 лет после взрыва сверхновой астрономы увидели, как её «сердце» — крошечная нейтронная звезда — зажигает вокруг себя радиотуманность. Если догадка верна, это первая внегалактическая колыбель пульсара, доступная для изучения.

title: Ice detectors listen to the radio voices of cosmic particles
oneliner: To catch neutrinos, scientists first learn to recognize signals from cosmic rays in Antarctic ice.
description: The ARA and RNO-G experiments in the ice of Antarctica and Greenland 'listen' for radio signals from neutrinos. But the main background comes from cosmic rays — their signals are almost identical. Using the FAERIE simulation, scientists have recreated the radio portrait of cosmic rays: wave directio
abstract: To hunt for neutrinos—ghost particles—antennas buried in the ice of Antarctica and Greenland pick up radio signals. But cosmic rays also leave similar traces, creating noise. Scientists simulated these radio signals to learn to tell the 'noise' from real neutrinos. It's like training your ear to catch a conversation in a noisy café.
threads: Космические лучи, влетая в антарктический лёд, порождают радиовспышки, похожие на следы нейтрино. Учёные создали симуляцию, чтобы как следует изучить эти помехи и в будущем безошибочно ловить частицы-призраки из глубин космоса.

title: Supernova Born from the Merger of Two Stars
oneliner: The most common type of supernova, it turns out, can arise from the merger of two stars.
description: Supernova SN 2018gj looked strange: it exploded in an old region of the galaxy and quickly faded. Computer modeling showed that such behavior can only be explained by the merger of two stars before the explosion. This is the first confirmation that the most massive type of supernovae does not always
threads: Астрономы нашли сверхновую, которая не вписывается в привычные схемы. Она взорвалась в очень старой галактике, а её свет гас слишком быстро. Оказалось, это след слияния двух звёзд — первый такой случай для самого распространённого типа сверхновых.

title: Galaxy Rotation in Voids Tests Inflation
oneliner: By observing the rotation of galaxies in cosmic voids, scientists have found a way to test exactly how the Universe inflated in its first moments.
description: In giant cosmic voids, galaxies spin like a single ensemble. Their dance creates a barely noticeable difference in glow from different sides. It turns out that this effect is the most accurate indicator of how uniformly matter was foamed up at the moment of inflation. It is enough to measure it to f
abstract: In vast cosmic voids, galaxies rotate like a single mechanism, like wood chips in a giant whirlpool. This rotation depends on the tiniest perturbations that arose at the birth of the Universe. By measuring it, we can check exactly how it expanded in the first fractions of a second. Thus, voids become a window into the inflationary epoch.
threads: Галактики в космических пустотах кружатся синхронно, как стая рыб. Малейшее отклонение в их танце выдаёт, как именно раздувалась Вселенная в первые мгновения. Этот метод может стать самым точным тестом теории инфляции.

title: Dark Energy: A Recipe with a Pinch of Salt
oneliner: New research shows: dark energy may contain a tiny negative fraction, like salt in sweet dough.
description: Analysis of DESI data showed that adding a tiny negative fraction to dark energy improves agreement with observations, making it almost perfectly constant. Like a pinch of salt in dough, this addition brings harmony to the universe's expansion.
abstract: Scientists explored the idea that the Universe might be governed by two components at once: the familiar dark energy (which pushes things apart) and a negative cosmological constant (which pulls things together, like a spring). After crunching the observational data, they found that this model fits the facts a bit better. Could it be that the cosmos is trickier than we thought?
threads: Что если тёмная энергия, заставляющая Вселенную расширяться всё быстрее, не однородна? Новые данные намекают на скрытую отрицательную часть — как космическую щепотку соли, делающую всю картину гармоничнее. Этот крохотный «противовес» может перевернуть наши представления о судьбе мироздания.

title: Icy Visitor from Another System
oneliner: The SPHEREx telescope caught an interstellar wanderer 'boiling' from solar heat, releasing clouds of vapor and organics.
description: ISO 3I/ATLAS is the second known interstellar object. As it approached the Sun, it bared its interior: its icy shell evaporated, revealing a mix of water, carbon dioxide, and carbon compounds. These findings help us understand the chemical makeup of other planetary systems.
abstract: In December 2025, NASA's SPHEREx space telescope studied interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS once again. After its close approach to the Sun, it became much more active: its ices began to evaporate like dry ice under sunlight, exposing carbon-rich materials. This helps us understand what visitors from other star systems are made of. What other surprises will this wanderer bring?
threads: Ледяной сгусток из иной звёздной системы подлетел к Солнцу и вдруг «зашипел», выбросив облака пара и даже сложной органики. Оказалось, там были спрятаны углеродные льды, которые смог обнажить только космический зонд. Теперь мы знаем: строительные блоки жизни могут переживать межзвёздные путешествия.

title: The Universe Falls Silent Smoothly, Not with a Bang
oneliner: A model with dark energy inertia predicts a soft halt to the expansion of the Universe.
description: Scientists have built a model where dark energy loses its push. Instead of a shredding tear or a crunch into a point, the Universe’s expansion smoothly tapers off. The end result — a flat, table-smooth, empty space. Observations back this up.
threads: Тёмная энергия сама себя тормозит! Новая модель наделяет её инерцией: расширение Вселенной затухает, как качели без толчка. Вместо разрыва или сжатия — вечная тишина: космос мягко замирает в плоской пустоте.

title: Lava Oceans of Exoplanets
oneliner: Tidal forces melt entire planets, forging lava oceans with waves kilometers high.
description: A planet on a slightly egg-shaped orbit swings in close to its star, then pulls away. The changing gravitational tug heats its guts, melting rock into a lava ocean. Tidal waves stir it, spawning wandering hot spots. This makes the planet's glow flicker unpredictably—and sometimes the entire world tu
abstract: Imagine a planet so close to its star that its dayside is an ocean of molten rock. But the star’s tidal forces slosh this ocean from within, creating giant lava waves. As these waves collide, they make the planet flicker unpredictably, like a candle flame in the wind.
threads: Есть планеты с океанами из раскалённого камня. Оказывается, если орбита чуть-чуть вытянута, то внутри всё плавится, а по лаве гуляют волны-гиганты. Они сталкиваются, создавая непредсказуемые пятна тепла. Такая планета мерцает, как неровное пламя, и может быть жидкой насквозь.

title: The Climate Swings of Ancient Mars
oneliner: The planet periodically heated up and was briefly covered with rivers and lakes.
description: A new study explains how Mars could have had water with a faint Sun: the climate swayed between long freezes and brief thaws, leaving behind riverbeds.
abstract: Imagine Mars' climate working like an ancient self-regulating furnace: heating up and cooling down in cycles. Research shows that such cycles could have created rivers and lakes 3-4 billion years ago. Could a similar mechanism explain other mysteries of the Red Planet?
threads: Сегодня Марс — ледяная пустыня, но 4 миллиарда лет назад там журчали ручьи. Парадокс в том, что Солнце тогда было тусклее. Разгадка — в климатических качелях: планета периодически разогревалась из-за углекислого газа. Это объясняет, как на холодном Марсе появлялась вода.

title: How Gravitational Waves Twist Light
oneliner: Gravitational waves can change the twist of photons — as if space itself rotates polarization.
description: Scientists have described for the first time a mechanism by which gravitational waves flip the twist of light. The effect resembles the action of sugar syrup on polarization, but here curved spacetime does the work. The exchange of spin between the wave and the photon is rigidly fixed: gravity's spi
abstract: Scientists have shown for the first time that gravitational waves can 'switch' the direction of light's rotation (its helicity), much like a huge wave changes the spin of a small float. This discovery gives us a new way to 'listen' to gravitational waves, picking up their hidden structure. What else will this cosmic dance of particles tell us?
threads: Обычный луч света, пролетая через рябь пространства, может неожиданно сменить свою закрученность — как если бы гравитационный шторм перевернул его поляризацию. Этот хитрый эффект позволит заглянуть в тайны чёрных дыр и самого рождения Вселенной.

title: Space is Colder Than We Hoped
oneliner: Astronomers conducted the largest search for thermal traces of extraterrestrial civilizations among thousands of galaxies and found nothing but a chill.
description: Scientists analyzed infrared data from the WISE telescope to detect possible Dyson spheres or other technologies that reprocess starlight. After excluding ordinary hot objects, they set an upper limit: only one in 6,500 galaxies could emit such excess heat. The probability of encountering an advance
abstract: Astronomers searched in infrared light for heat that highly advanced civilizations could emit (like the heat of a giant data center) in nearby galaxies. It turned out that such 'technogenic' glow is incredibly faint — no more than a couple percent of ordinary starlight. Are we really alone if there are no obvious traces of intelligence?
threads: Спросите у сковороды: после готовки всегда остаётся нагрев. Астрономы «прощупали» тысячи галактик в инфракрасных лучах — ищут внеземной разум по лишнему теплу. Выяснилось: если кто-то и превращает целые звёзды в обогреватели, такие почти незаметны. Космос подозрительно холоден.

title: The Universe Whispers: [tag:dark_energy]dark energy[/tag] is not alone
oneliner: Data from the DESI survey hints that dark energy and dark matter may be having a secret dialogue.
description: Scientists have long debated: is dark energy constant or does it change? Fresh measurements point to a third path — it is conversing with dark matter. This turns a lone cosmic force into a duet of invisible interlocutors.
abstract: Recent data on baryon acoustic oscillations from the DESI telescope showed a mismatch with the standard cosmological model. It turns out this can be explained by an interaction between dark matter and dark energy, not just 'dynamic' dark energy. Like dance partners, they influence each other, and this approach explains the observations just as well. So who’s really steering the expansion of the universe?
threads: Космологи долго спорили: тёмная энергия постоянна или меняется? Новые данные подсказывают третий путь — она общается с тёмной материей. Это как если бы ветер не просто дул в парус, но и сам парус решал, куда плыть. Открытие может перевернуть наше понимание Вселенной.

title: Dark Matter from the Bends of Space
oneliner: A sharp change in curvature after inflation could have created all the dark matter in the universe.
description: After rapid expansion (inflation), spacetime quickly restructures, triggering instability in the dark matter field. This kicks off an avalanche of particle creation — a purely gravitational mechanism. Calculations and computer simulations confirm that this can explain the observed density of dark ma
abstract: After the Big Bang, space stretched rapidly. This stretching could have acted like a pump, 'shaking' invisible particles and turning them into the dark matter that fills the universe. Imagine a calm pond that suddenly ripples and splashes water in all directions—could it be that the very shape of space created the hidden mass that holds galaxies together?
threads: После Большого взрыва пространство искривилось так резко, что «вытряхнуло» из вакуума тёмную материю. Симуляции показали: такой механизм даёт ровно столько невидимого вещества, сколько видят астрономы.

title: Why Black Holes Can't Merge Without Gas
oneliner: Star collisions can't bring supermassive black holes together — only cosmic gas drives them to merge.
description: Astrophysicists discovered that stars alone aren't enough for supermassive black holes to merge — gas plays the decisive role. It not only brings the pair together but also makes them shine, revealing the merger long before the finale.
abstract: What brings pairs of supermassive black holes at the centers of galaxies together? Scientists tested whether 'kicks' from stars alone could do the job. It turns out, stars aren't enough—you also need gas 'lubrication'. So, mergers might be accompanied by bright flares visible through telescopes. Imagine two colossal cosmic whirlpools that just can't merge without an extra push from a gas current.
threads: Сверхмассивные чёрные дыры в центрах галактик сближаются, как танцоры в толпе. Но чтобы начать вальс, им нужен не только толчок звёзд, но и газовое облако. Иначе вселенский гравитационный гул звучал бы иначе. А это ключ к разгадке роста галактик.

title: How to Hear a Swarm of Black Holes
oneliner: By the hum of gravitational waves, you can tell if it was born from a swarm of primordial black holes.
description: Future detectors will catch the hum from mergers of tiny black holes. New work shows how to distinguish this hum from other sources by its volume and frequency, and peer into the first second after the Big Bang.
abstract: Imagine you’re listening to a forest: you can pick out individual footsteps (rare signals) or the hum of many sounds blending together. Scientists have proposed using the same principle to distinguish special black holes from other sources of gravitational waves—by how intermittent or continuous the “stream” of signals is. This will help new detectors more accurately identify who’s making the background cosmic noise. And could you tell from the sound how many people are walking through the forest?
threads: Крошечные чёрные дыры, рождённые в самом начале Вселенной, могут создавать космический гул, похожий на шум дорожной пробки. Учёные поняли, как по характеру этого гула – его громкости и частоте – надёжно вычислить эти древние объекты и заглянуть в младенчество мироздания.

title: Dark Matter and Its Taste Preferences
oneliner: Physicists have figured out which 'ingredients' of ordinary matter dark matter prefers to interact with.
description: Like a fussy chef, dark matter can only 'cook' the Universe using certain particles. New work shows how strongly it prefers quarks (the building blocks of atomic nuclei) over leptons (electrons and their kin). Using the idea of culinary symmetries and their violations, scientists estimated how far o
abstract: Scientists have studied how the interaction of dark matter with ordinary particles might be arranged. They found out which options do not contradict observations, and calculated how much one can deviate from symmetric models. This will help future experiments in the search for traces of dark matter. Imagine that dark matter is an invisible key that opens only certain locks in the microworld.
threads: Тёмная материя как привередливый повар: она «готовит» Вселенную только из определённых частиц. Физики выяснили, насколько сильно можно нарушить эту кулинарную симметрию, чтобы не испортить блюдо. Возможно, скоро детекторы уловят её «фирменный рецепт».

title: Star Duet Spawns Ripples in Spacetime
oneliner: Astronomers have discovered a cosmic duet whose accelerating dance shakes the fabric of space.
description: A pair of white dwarfs whirls in a deadly dance: one star steals helium from its neighbor, spinning up a glowing disk. The orbit shrinks, energy leaves as gravitational waves. The rate of approach has been measured — the future LISA detector will catch the signal in four years.
abstract: Astronomers found a pair of dead stars whirling around each other in just 8.5 minutes — faster than you can read this sentence. One star steals matter from the other, their orbit shrinks, and spacetime ripples are born, like rings from a stone dropped in water. A space detector will soon hear this ringing. Imagine: a distant bell tolling the tale of dying stars.
threads: Обнаружен удивительный звёздный дуэт: каждые 8,5 минут одна звезда затмевает другую. Энергия улетает гравитационными волнами — рябью пространства. Через несколько лет их «услышит» космический детектор LISA. Это как камертон вселенной, звучащий в глубинах космоса.

title: When the Sun Dived into a Cloud
oneliner: Beryllium-10 traces from cosmic showers reveal when the Solar System trekked through dense clouds.
description: When the Solar System enters dense interstellar clouds, the Sun's protective bubble shrinks. The intensified flow of cosmic rays spawns beryllium-10 in the atmosphere, which settles in ocean sediments and ice. Studying these layers uncovers when and which clouds the Earth encountered, letting us rea
threads: Представьте, что Солнце заходит в огромное облако из пыли и газа — наш защитный пузырь сжимается до орбиты Земли, и начинается невидимый ливень космических лучей. В воздухе рождается радиоактивный бериллий, который падает в океан и застывает во льдах. Следы такого «душа» нашли в породах возрастом 10 млн лет — это не взрыв сверхновой, а встреча с космическим облаком.

title: The Colors of Life on Distant Planets
oneliner: The future HWO telescope will track down life by color patterns on faraway worlds
description: Scientists have figured out how to search for life on other planets by looking for colored traces on the surface. These paints are left by living organisms. The HWO telescope will spot such color signatures even if the air shows no signs of life — for instance, when it’s ancient and doesn’t produce
threads: Астроном из другой системы, взглянув на Землю, заметил бы зеленоватый оттенок от её лесов — поверхностный биосигнал. Похожим образом будущий телескоп HWO будет искать «краски» бактерий и растений на далёких мирах. Даже если атмосфера не выдаёт тайны, цвет поверхности может рассказать о древней жизни.

title: The Mystery of Cosmic Dust: Weighing a Galaxy
oneliner: Webb data made astronomers recalculate the mass of early galaxies: now it's a bit less, but still mind-blowingly huge.
description: Astronomers refined the mass of three extremely distant galaxies using Webb, and it turned out: invisible dust can distort our estimates more than we imagined, but even with corrections, two galaxies remain monstrously massive for their young age.
abstract: New data from the James Webb Space Telescope forced a re-evaluation of the masses of three heavyweight galaxies from the early Universe. It turns out one of them is closer than previously thought, and the final numbers are heavily influenced by assumptions about dust and star formation rates—like colored glasses through which we view distant light. So the mystery of the super-efficient star factories remains unsolved for now.
threads: Толстый слой космической пыли мешает точно взвесить далёкие галактики. Новые данные с телескопа Вебба показали: меняя модель пыли и роста, массу можно занизить вдвое. Три галактики-«тяжеловеса» слегка полегчали, но две всё равно выбиваются из теории. Возможно, ранняя Вселенная растила звёзды быстрее, чем мы думали.

title: A Spinning Top at the Galaxy’s Core Snuffs Out Stars
oneliner: A wobbling black hole ejects gas, robbing the galaxy of new stars.
description: Scientists studied the galaxy VV 340a, where the jet from its central black hole wobbles with a period of about 800,000 years. It sweeps away gas—roughly 19 solar masses per year. The superheated gas leaves the galaxy, and star formation shuts down.
abstract: Scientists studied the galaxy VV 340a, where a jet from a black hole, wobbling, blows away gas — like a fan that blows away flour, preventing you from making pastries. The jet wobbles with a period of 820 thousand years and carries away 19 solar masses of gas per year. This slows down star birth: does the 'fan' at the galaxy's center control its life?
threads: Чёрная дыра в центре галактики VV 340a ведёт себя как космический волчок: её струя качается и выбрасывает газ со скоростью 19 масс Солнца в год. Лишившись строительного материала, галактика перестаёт создавать новые звёзды.

title: The Mystery of Asteroid Kamo'oalewa: Why It's Not from the Moon
oneliner: Asteroid Kamo'oalewa most likely comes from the main asteroid belt, not the Moon.
description: Astronomers have unraveled the origin of Kamo'oalewa. The lunar shard hypothesis hasn't held up: there are dozens of times more similar bodies from the asteroid belt. The Chinese Tianwen-2 mission will settle the matter.
abstract: Asteroid Kamoʻoalewa is the target of a Chinese sample-return mission. Scientists debated: is it a chunk of the Moon or an interloper from the main belt? Calculations showed: the probability of a lunar origin is minuscule; most likely, it's from the asteroid belt. So who is it — a child of the Moon or a wanderer?
threads: Астероид Камоалева долго считали осколком Луны — его спектр уж очень похож на лунный грунт. Но новый подсчёт показал: объектов из пояса астероидов с такими орбитами в десятки раз больше. Так что это, скорее всего, обычный космический странник, а не сын Селены. Тяньвэнь-2 проверит.

title: Primordial Black Holes Build Early Galaxies
oneliner: Tiny black holes born moments after the Big Bang may have jump-started the formation of giant black holes in the young universe.
description: The James Webb Space Telescope has spotted enormous black holes in the young universe that couldn't have grown in the usual way. Their secret might be primordial black holes—tiny objects from the universe's first moments. Playing the role of dark matter, they rapidly merged at the centers of galaxie
abstract: Recently, deep in space, astronomers found the galaxy UHZ1: its stars and central black hole are nearly equal in mass. Scientists suspect they were crafted by primordial black holes — 'seeds' of dark matter. They clumped together, and the heaviest ones sank to the center, like sand in a funnel. That's how an enormous black hole grew. Could this be how other mysterious early-Universe objects came to be?
threads: Телескоп Уэбба разглядел в юной Вселенной чёрные дыры-гиганты, которые не должны были успеть вырасти. Их секрет может быть в первичных чёрных дырах — крошечных объектах из первого мгновения мира, которые быстро слипались в центрах галактик. Такая картина указывает на природу тёмной материи.

title: Hidden Gas Clumps Trick Astronomers in Early Galaxies
oneliner: James Webb Space Telescope reveals that unseen dense gas knots can throw off measurements of ancient galaxies’ ingredients.
description: JWST studied three distant galaxies and found tiny, dense gas clumps dominate certain light signals. This makes standard methods underestimate elements like oxygen and nitrogen. New computer models that treat gas more realistically fix these errors, helping us understand galaxy formation.
threads: Tiny, dense gas clumps—like blueberries hidden in a muffin—can trick our best telescopes. In early galaxies, these knots shine so brightly they make the whole galaxy seem chemically poorer. This cosmic illusion means we might be misreading the history of stars and elements.

title: Echo of a Silenced Black Hole
oneliner: Light from the center of galaxy GSN 069, like an echo, brought news: the giant black hole was recently active but has now fallen asleep.
description: Astronomers measured the polarization of light from GSN 069—the direction in which its waves oscillate. The farther from the center, the more ordered the light became. This pointed to an extinguished active nucleus: the black hole once blazed brightly, and distant clouds still reflect its former bri
abstract: Scientists studied a galaxy where mysterious X-ray 'bursts' occur. They found that light from its center behaves like an echo from a switched-off flashlight: polarization (the directionality of light waves) gets stronger farther from the core, showing that it once shone brightly but has now faded. It seems the ancient active 'heart' of the galaxy left behind a glowing, misty trail. What else might these cosmic echoes reveal?
threads: Поляризованный свет из центра галактики выдал тайну: чёрная дыра-монстр там уже выключилась, но её былую активность всё ещё видно в отражениях. Так астрономы заглядывают в прошлое Вселенной.

title: Dark Energy as a Spring: A Model with No Tuning
oneliner: Dark energy turned out to be not an eternal constant, but a weakening spring. And no fudge factors—everything is dictated by fundamental theory.
description: Astronomers tested a model where dark energy is not a constant force, but a spring gradually losing its tension. The most surprising part: that same spring also explains dark matter, and all properties are set by fundamental theory without a single free parameter. Data from the DESI, Planck, and sup
threads: Тёмную энергию, возможно, порождает невидимое поле, которое в ранней Вселенной вело себя как жёсткая пружина, а теперь плавно ослабевает. Это объясняет ускоряющееся расширение без единого произвольного параметра — все числа диктуются фундаментальной теорией.

title: Stellar Bakeries: Two Suns, One Organic Recipe
oneliner: Two young stars in the same cradle are surrounded by a strikingly similar set of organic molecules.
description: The ALMA radio telescope peered into the triple system HOPS-288, where three stars are being born. Around two of them, a chemical kitchen is bubbling: warm clouds where complex molecules assemble from icy dust. Astonishingly, the list of these molecules is almost identical for both stars — as if pre
abstract: Astronomers peered into the 'cradle' of two newborn stars, located 200 astronomical units apart. There they found a veritable 'soup' of complex organic molecules – precursors of life. Remarkably, the chemical makeup of both stars turned out to be almost identical, as if they inherited the same ingredients from the parent cloud. Could this be how the 'recipe for life' gets passed down in star systems?
threads: В 200 астрономических единицах друг от друга две новорожденные звезды окружены одинаковым набором сложных органических молекул — как будто из одного теста. Это намекает на то, что условия для зарождения жизни могут быть стандартными в двойных системах.

title: A hiccup in the early Universe gave rise to a gravitational hum
oneliner: A brief pause in the expansion of the newborn Universe might have created a burst of gravitational waves detectable by Earth-based detectors.
description: In the first moments after the Big Bang, the Universe expanded at an enormous speed. If the expansion briefly paused, it generated powerful gravitational waves. Future detectors on Earth will be able to catch them, opening a window into an era hidden from any telescopes.
abstract: In the first moments, the universe inflated at a mind-boggling speed. If this process hit a pause button just for an instant, it sent ripples through spacetime — gravitational waves. Scientists have shown that these ripples can be especially strong and detectable by new, ultra-sensitive instruments on Earth. Imagine: we could reach out and touch the dawn of time!
threads: Неожиданная остановка в раздувании новорождённой Вселенной могла оставить всплеск гравитационных волн. Сегодня они растянулись до частот, доступных земным детекторам вроде Einstein Telescope. Короткая пауза в космическом рывке — и у нас появится ключ к событиям сразу после Большого взрыва.

title: Cosmic Billiards: How Planets Become Wanderers
oneliner: Massive outer planets eject small worlds into interstellar space, creating rogue planets.
description: Gravitational kicks from distant giant planets knock small rocky worlds (super-Earths) near the star out of the system. The ejected planet becomes a lonely wanderer. This same mechanism can shuffle the orbits of the remaining planets and even send them plunging into the star.
threads: Представьте бильярдный стол космических масштабов: массивные внешние планеты, как тяжёлые шары, выталкивают лёгкие внутренние миры за пределы системы. Так рождаются планеты-изгои — вечные скитальцы межзвёздной тьмы. Их открытие меняет наше понимание планетарной эволюции.

title: A Black Hole with an Icy Surface
oneliner: A new model describes a hole that freezes everything at its boundary and shines from within its shadow.
description: Scientists have proposed a model of a black hole where matter and light freeze at the edge, like a whirlpool gripped by ice. Its shadow betrays itself with unexpected brightness—a path to testing quantum gravity.
abstract: Imagine a black hole where spacetime itself changes its properties right at the horizon, and infalling light never reaches the center. Scientists studied its shadow and noticed an unusually bright spot inside—like a flashlight in the dark. This might help us see how the black hole horizon is truly structured. What other secrets do such bizarre objects hide?
threads: Обычная чёрная дыра засасывает всё, что падает за её край. Но учёные описали дыру, которая на этом краю превращается в непробиваемую стену, и свет не может упасть в центр. Такую дыру можно заметить — в её тени прячется яркое свечение.

title: The Cosmic Engine Overheated
oneliner: A single field couldn't explain both inflation and dark energy—spacetime ripples got in the way.
description: Physicists suspected that both the inflation of the young Universe and its current accelerated expansion were driven by the same field—like a single engine running in different modes. But telescope data showed that switching modes produces powerful spacetime ripples that overheated the ancient cosmo
